title: tilia platyphyllos subsp. platyphyllos
intro: Tilia platyphyllos subsp. platyphyllos, also known as the large-leaved linden, is a subspecies of the Tilia platyphyllos tree native to Europe and parts of Asia. The large-leaved linden is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties, making it useful for treating anxiety, insomnia, and skin issues. The health benefits of the large-leaved linden include reducing stress and promoting relaxation, as well as its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, such as coughs and sore throats. Herbal preparations of the large-leaved linden include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make a soothing tea or applied topically to the skin to aid in wound healing and skin repair.
